description: A struct field typed as a function type fires E-STRUCT-FUNCTION-FIELD (§14.3, S174 ruling). The field `cb: fn()` stores behavior on value data; a function has no structural equality, is not serializable, and cannot be a map key, so it SHALL NOT be a struct field — the type system rejects the declaration. Incidental W-PROGRAM-SPA-INFERRED is ignored by superset matching.
rationale: §14.3 (limit-the-primitive axiom §14.1.1) a struct is a collection of data and state, NOT a behavior-bearing object; a function-typed field is the STORED face of the passed-vs-stored rule and is a hard compile error (E-STRUCT-FUNCTION-FIELD). Model behavior with an enum tag or an engine.
